summary: Release Verification Recovery Fix: transparent-status HUD and closed-fist hand-guide checks are independently validated, so a valid release no longer fails on a mismatched verifier.
features: Final release validation now invokes the dedicated transparent status-HUD contract instead of mistakenly reusing the fist-guide verifier
The visible hand guide no longer depends on the open-hand drawing-eligibility gate, so a confidently detected closed fist keeps its landmarks and links visible
Closed-fist guide rendering uses the same normalized camera crop and mirror projection as an open hand
A short bounded guide hold covers one or two detector drop frames during finger occlusion without keeping a stale hand overlay indefinitely
Drawing and pinch safety remain strict: a closed fist never becomes an accidental drawing action merely because the guide is visible
Hand detector confidence is relaxed only for the detector; geometry, stability and pinch gates retain their existing safeguards
FPS, Online and CAM remain text-only overlays with no background panel, border or blur surface
Local MediaPipe GPU delegate remains preferred with deterministic CPU fallback
